entries.definition:
sh–r–r sparks of fire; evil, to be or become evil, to be depraved, to be wicked; to slander; to cleave, to split, to sharpen. Of this root, three forms occur 31 times in the Qurʾan: شر sharr 29 times; أشرار ashrār once and شرر sharar once.
شر sharr I [elat.] more/most evil, worse, worst (22:72) أَفَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِشَرٍّ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمُ النَّارُ shall I tell you what is worse than that?, the Fire II [n.] evil, wickedness (76:7) يُوفُونَ بِالنَّذْرِ وَيَخَافُونَ يَوْمًا كَانَ شَرُّهُ مُسْتَطِيرًا they fulfil their vows and fear a day whose evil is spreading wide.

481

شر sharr I [elat.] more/most evil, worse, worst (22:72) أَفَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِشَرٍّ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمُ النَّارُ shall I tell you what is worse than that?, the Fire II [n.] evil, wickedness (76:7) يُوفُونَ بِالنَّذْرِ وَيَخَافُونَ يَوْمًا كَانَ شَرُّهُ مُسْتَطِيرًا they fulfil their vows and fear a day whose evil is spreading wide.

481
